requirements.title: Agent-facing entrypoints shall state that generated stubs are trace slots and not validation evidence.

statements.title: Agent-facing entrypoints shall state that generated stubs are trace slots and not validation evidence.
statements.text: Agent-facing entrypoints shall state that generated stubs are trace slots and not validation evidence.

requirements.title: When an agent implements accepted behavior, the agent protocol shall require the governing statement IDs and verification plan before or alongside code changes.

statements.title: When an agent implements accepted behavior, the agent protocol shall require the governing statement IDs and verification plan before or alongside code changes.
statements.text: When an agent implements accepted behavior, the agent protocol shall require the governing statement IDs and verification plan before or alongside code changes.

requirements.title: If a review finding lacks authority, release scope, implementation evidence, or core-journey impact, then agent-facing guidance shall not classify it as a release blocker.

statements.title: If a review finding lacks authority, release scope, implementation evidence, or core-journey impact, then agent-facing guidance shall not classify it as a release blocker.
statements.text: If a review finding lacks authority, release scope, implementation evidence, or core-journey impact, then agent-facing guidance shall not classify it as a release blocker.

requirements.title: Agent-facing guidance shall state that accepted specs are product standards, not implementation inventories.

statements.title: Agent-facing guidance shall state that accepted specs are product standards, not implementation inventories.
statements.text: Agent-facing guidance shall state that accepted specs are product standards, not implementation inventories.

statements.title: If reviewed implementation lacks accepted spec behavior, then agent-facing guidance shall keep the spec and classify an evidence gap instead of downgrading the requirement.
statements.text: If reviewed implementation lacks accepted spec behavior, then agent-facing guidance shall keep the spec and classify an evidence gap instead of downgrading the requirement.

statements.title: When spec and code disagree, the agent protocol shall use the gap taxonomy: `missing_implementation`, `partial_implementation`, `missing_test`, `wrong_spec`, `wrong_code`, and `decision_gap`.
statements.text: When spec and code disagree, the agent protocol shall use the gap taxonomy: `missing_implementation`, `partial_implementation`, `missing_test`, `wrong_spec`, `wrong_code`, and `decision_gap`.

requirements.title: When applying a new ILS version, upstream rule, or template to an existing project, agent-facing guidance shall require both governance install and feature-spec propagation audit before reporting completion.

statements.title: When applying a new ILS version, upstream rule, or template to an existing project, agent-facing guidance shall require both governance install and feature-spec propagation audit before reporting completion.
statements.text: When applying a new ILS version, upstream rule, or template to an existing project, agent-facing guidance shall require both governance install and feature-spec propagation audit before reporting completion.

statements.title: If any in-scope authoritative spec was not reviewed under the new method rule, then the final status shall be `partial` and the unreviewed files shall be recorded as `pending_spec_review`.
statements.text: If any in-scope authoritative spec was not reviewed under the new method rule, then the final status shall be `partial` and the unreviewed files shall be recorded as `pending_spec_review`.

statements.title: Method-update reports shall list the upstream rule/version, authoritative spec inventory, reviewed specs, excluded specs, residual gaps, generated artifact updates, and verification commands.
statements.text: Method-update reports shall list the upstream rule/version, authoritative spec inventory, reviewed specs, excluded specs, residual gaps, generated artifact updates, and verification commands.

requirements.title: Agent-facing guidance shall route broad or ambiguous user requests into a named task mode before acting.

statements.title: Agent-facing guidance shall route broad or ambiguous user requests into a named task mode before acting.
statements.text: Agent-facing guidance shall route broad or ambiguous user requests into a named task mode before acting.

statements.title: When a request asks to apply a latest ILS version, upstream rule, or new template, the agent shall select Method update mode and follow its propagation completion rule.
statements.text: When a request asks to apply a latest ILS version, upstream rule, or new template, the agent shall select Method update mode and follow its propagation completion rule.

statements.title: If the selected mode's completion rule is not satisfied, then the agent shall report `partial`, `blocked`, `unverified`, or `manual_only` instead of claiming `complete`.
statements.text: If the selected mode's completion rule is not satisfied, then the agent shall report `partial`, `blocked`, `unverified`, or `manual_only` instead of claiming `complete`.

requirements.title: Agent-facing guides and templates shall require feature archetype packs before implementation so predictable edge cases are considered by feature type.

statements.title: Agent-facing guides and templates shall require feature archetype packs before implementation so predictable edge cases are considered by feature type.
statements.text: Agent-facing guides and templates shall require feature archetype packs before implementation so predictable edge cases are considered by feature type.

requirements.title: If a feature accepts valid user input and an automation step can fail, then the authoring guidance shall require input preservation and a recoverable draft, still-processing state, retry path, or actionable error instead of an empty manual-only fallback.

statements.title: If a feature accepts valid user input and an automation step can fail, then the authoring guidance shall require input preservation and a recoverable draft, still-processing state, retry path, or actionable error instead of an empty manual-only fallback.
statements.text: If a feature accepts valid user input and an automation step can fail, then the authoring guidance shall require input preservation and a recoverable draft, still-processing state, retry path, or actionable error instead of an empty manual-only fallback.

requirements.title: If customer-visible processing can outlive the generic API timeout, then the authoring guidance shall require a latency contract: synchronous, endpoint-specific long request, polling, background job, or streaming.

statements.title: If customer-visible processing can outlive the generic API timeout, then the authoring guidance shall require a latency contract: synchronous, endpoint-specific long request, polling, background job, or streaming.
statements.text: If customer-visible processing can outlive the generic API timeout, then the authoring guidance shall require a latency contract: synchronous, endpoint-specific long request, polling, background job, or streaming.

requirements.title: If every requirement in a meaningful customer-facing feature has exactly one behavior statement, then the tooling or checklist shall flag under-decomposition review rather than treating equal REQ/EARS counts as automatically healthy.

statements.title: If every requirement in a meaningful customer-facing feature has exactly one behavior statement, then the tooling or checklist shall flag under-decomposition review rather than treating equal REQ/EARS counts as automatically healthy.
statements.text: If every requirement in a meaningful customer-facing feature has exactly one behavior statement, then the tooling or checklist shall flag under-decomposition review rather than treating equal REQ/EARS counts as automatically healthy.

requirements.title: When repository authoring rules change, the check suite shall verify that the README, guide, templates, and agent-facing entrypoints keep those rules visible.

statements.title: When repository authoring rules change, the check suite shall verify that the README, guide, templates, and agent-facing entrypoints keep those rules visible.
statements.text: When repository authoring rules change, the check suite shall verify that the README, guide, templates, and agent-facing entrypoints keep those rules visible.

requirements.title: When `spec/**/*.md` or `examples/**/spec.md` contains an EARS requirement, the generator shall assign a statement ID and create a generated skipped test slot.

statements.title: When `spec/**/*.md` or `examples/**/spec.md` contains an EARS requirement, the generator shall assign a statement ID and create a generated skipped test slot.
statements.text: When `spec/**/*.md` or `examples/**/spec.md` contains an EARS requirement, the generator shall assign a statement ID and create a generated skipped test slot.
statements.plannedVerification: guardrail | `npm run check:reqs` | `npm run check` | verified

requirements.title: The generated verification report shall separate generated-only pending statements from non-generated `@Spec(...)` references.

statements.title: The generated verification report shall separate generated-only pending statements from non-generated `@Spec(...)` references.
statements.text: The generated verification report shall separate generated-only pending statements from non-generated `@Spec(...)` references.

requirements.title: If a non-generated `@Spec(...)` reference points to an unknown requirement or broad multi-statement requirement, then the requirement check shall fail.

statements.title: If a non-generated `@Spec(...)` reference points to an unknown requirement or broad multi-statement requirement, then the requirement check shall fail.
statements.text: If a non-generated `@Spec(...)` reference points to an unknown requirement or broad multi-statement requirement, then the requirement check shall fail.
statements.plannedVerification: guardrail | `npm run check:reqs` | `npm run check` | verified

requirements.title: When a real project test verifies project behavior, the test shall reference the governing statement ID with `@Spec(...)`.

statements.title: When a real project test verifies project behavior, the test shall reference the governing statement ID with `@Spec(...)`.
statements.text: When a real project test verifies project behavior, the test shall reference the governing statement ID with `@Spec(...)`.

requirements.title: The system shall allow maximum one coupon on an `order_id`.

statements.title: The system shall allow maximum one coupon on an `order_id`.
statements.text: The system shall allow maximum one coupon on an `order_id`.

requirements.title: When the user applies a `coupon_id`, the system shall validate expiry, used state, and minimum order amount.

statements.title: When the user applies a `coupon_id`, the system shall validate expiry, used state, and minimum order amount.
statements.text: When the user applies a `coupon_id`, the system shall validate expiry, used state, and minimum order amount.

requirements.title: While an order is `draft`, the system shall allow the user to apply coupon and remove coupon.

statements.title: While an order is `draft`, the system shall allow the user to apply coupon and remove coupon.
statements.text: While an order is `draft`, the system shall allow the user to apply coupon and remove coupon.

requirements.title: If the user submits an already used coupon, then the system shall return a rejection error.

statements.title: If the user submits an already used coupon, then the system shall return a rejection error.
statements.text: If the user submits an already used coupon, then the system shall return a rejection error.

requirements.title: If item removal makes `total_amount` fall below the minimum order amount after coupon application, then the system shall detach the coupon and notify the user.

statements.title: If item removal makes `total_amount` fall below the minimum order amount after coupon application, then the system shall detach the coupon and notify the user.
statements.text: If item removal makes `total_amount` fall below the minimum order amount after coupon application, then the system shall detach the coupon and notify the user.

requirements.title: If an order is cancelled, then the system shall restore the coupon to unused state.

statements.title: If an order is cancelled, then the system shall restore the coupon to unused state.
statements.text: If an order is cancelled, then the system shall restore the coupon to unused state.

requirements.title: If a `cancelled` order is submitted for confirmation, then the system shall reject the transition.

statements.title: If a `cancelled` order is submitted for confirmation, then the system shall reject the transition.
statements.text: If a `cancelled` order is submitted for confirmation, then the system shall reject the transition.

requirements.title: If a discount would make `total_amount` negative, then the system shall set `total_amount` to zero and report the adjustment.

statements.title: If a discount would make `total_amount` negative, then the system shall set `total_amount` to zero and report the adjustment.
statements.text: If a discount would make `total_amount` negative, then the system shall set `total_amount` to zero and report the adjustment.

requirements.title: Where a premium entitlement is present, the system shall apply the premium discount after coupon validation.

statements.title: Where a premium entitlement is present, the system shall apply the premium discount after coupon validation.
statements.text: Where a premium entitlement is present, the system shall apply the premium discount after coupon validation.
statements.plannedVerification: Generated requirement stub; premium entitlement adapter test

requirements.title: GitHub Pages primary navigation shall expose only curated onboarding and evidence surfaces, not every template, internal experiment, or generated page.

statements.title: GitHub Pages primary navigation shall expose only curated onboarding and evidence surfaces, not every template, internal experiment, or generated page.
statements.text: GitHub Pages primary navigation shall expose only curated onboarding and evidence surfaces, not every template, internal experiment, or generated page.

requirements.title: When repository discovery metadata changes, the package metadata and public docs shall keep the same repository and homepage targets.

statements.title: When repository discovery metadata changes, the package metadata and public docs shall keep the same repository and homepage targets.
statements.text: When repository discovery metadata changes, the package metadata and public docs shall keep the same repository and homepage targets.

requirements.title: If public experiment results are shared, then the README shall state that the results are diagnostic checks and not universal model benchmarks.

statements.title: If public experiment results are shared, then the README shall state that the results are diagnostic checks and not universal model benchmarks.
statements.text: If public experiment results are shared, then the README shall state that the results are diagnostic checks and not universal model benchmarks.

requirements.title: The repository shall keep project-governing behavior in `spec/` before using that behavior as an implementation or release rule.

statements.title: The repository shall keep project-governing behavior in `spec/` before using that behavior as an implementation or release rule.
statements.text: The repository shall keep project-governing behavior in `spec/` before using that behavior as an implementation or release rule.

requirements.title: When a project-governing behavior changes, the repository shall update the relevant feature spec and mapped verification in the same change.

statements.title: When a project-governing behavior changes, the repository shall update the relevant feature spec and mapped verification in the same change.
statements.text: When a project-governing behavior changes, the repository shall update the relevant feature spec and mapped verification in the same change.

requirements.title: If a generated artifact becomes stale after a spec change, then the repository check shall fail until the artifact is regenerated.

statements.title: If a generated artifact becomes stale after a spec change, then the repository check shall fail until the artifact is regenerated.
statements.text: If a generated artifact becomes stale after a spec change, then the repository check shall fail until the artifact is regenerated.

requirements.title: The package version shall match the latest changelog release heading.

statements.title: The package version shall match the latest changelog release heading.
statements.text: The package version shall match the latest changelog release heading.

requirements.title: The package manifest shall expose repository, homepage, bugs, license, keywords, and package file boundaries for public reuse.

statements.title: The package manifest shall expose repository, homepage, bugs, license, keywords, and package file boundaries for public reuse.
statements.text: The package manifest shall expose repository, homepage, bugs, license, keywords, and package file boundaries for public reuse.

requirements.title: When a release is published, the release tag shall use `v<package version>` and the changelog entry shall describe the released behavior.

requirements.title: Session setup after identity callback

statements.title: Session setup after identity callback
statements.text: When identity authorization succeeds, the system shall exchange the authorization result for an `account_id`, create a local `session_id`, and redirect to the validated `callback_url`.
statements.plannedVerification: Integration test for successful callback exchange

statements.title: Session setup after identity callback
statements.text: If local account setup fails after identity authorization succeeds, the system shall show a recoverable setup error and shall not create an `active` session.
statements.plannedVerification: Integration test for recoverable setup failure
